Question:
We will perform a logistic regression using NHANES data from 2009-2016.
Include participants whose age at screening 18 years or more and who have diabetes, defined as meeting any of these: who said yes to self-reported diaetes or takes diabetic medication or takes insulin
Exclude who are missing neutrophil counts or lymphocyte counts.
Exclude participants missing any of the depression questions: "have little interest in doing things", "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless", "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much", "feeling tired or having little energy", "poor appetite or overeating", "feeling bad about yourself", "trouble concentrating on things", "moving or speaking slowly or too fast", "thought you would be better off dead".
The main predictor is neutrophil count / lymphocyte count, categorize in quartiles(%) Q1: ≤25, Q2: >25 and ≤50, Q3: >50 and ≤75, Q4: >75 and adjust for age at screening, gender, and race/Hispanic origin.
Outcome is depression outcome, which is based on the total score from the nine questions were calculated where each question has value of Not at all (0), Several days (1), More than half the days (2), Nearly every day (3), if value is null assign (0), and the total score >=10 was defined as clinically relevant depression and else was not.
Structured Question:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2009-2016

Inclusion Criteria:
- age at screening >= 18 years
- diabetes defined as meeting any of these:
  self-reported diabetes = Yes OR
  takes diabetic medication = Yes OR
  takes insulin = Yes

Exclusion Criteria:
- missing neutrophil counts
- missing lymphocyte counts
- missing any of the depression questions:
  "have little interest in doing things"
  "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
  "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
  "feeling tired or having little energy"
  "poor appetite or overeating"
  "feeling bad about yourself"
  "trouble concentrating on things"
  "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
  "thought you would be better off dead"

Covariates:
- age at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- neutrophil count / lymphocyte count, categorized in quartiles:
  Q1: ≤25%
  Q2: >25% and ≤50%
  Q3: >50% and ≤75%
  Q4: >75%

Outcome:
- depression outcome = 1 if total score >= 10; 0 otherwise
  where total score is calculated from nine depression questions:
  "have little interest in doing things"
  "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
  "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
  "feeling tired or having little energy"
  "poor appetite or overeating"
  "feeling bad about yourself"
  "trouble concentrating on things"
  "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
  "thought you would be better off dead"
  Each question scored as:
  Not at all (0)
  Several days (1)
  More than half the days (2)
  Nearly every day (3)
  If value is null assign (0)

-- Add index to the main ID table for the final join

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t1."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t2."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar",
    t3."taking_insulin_now",
    t4."segmented_neutrophils_num_1000_cell_ul",
    t5."lymphocyte_number_1000_cells_ul",
    t6."have_little_interest_in_doing_things",
    t7."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless",
    t8."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much",
    t9."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy",
    t10."poor_appetite_or_overeating",
    t11."feeling_bad_about_yourself",
    t12."trouble_concentrating_on_things",
    t13."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast",
    t14."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead",
    t15."gender",
    t16."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_taking_insulin_now t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_segmented_neutrophils_num_1000_cell_ul t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_lymphocyte_number_1000_cells_ul t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_have_little_interest_in_doing_things t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy t9 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t9.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_poor_appetite_or_overeating t10 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t10.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_feeling_bad_about_yourself t11 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t11.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_trouble_concentrating_on_things t12 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t12.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast t13 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t13.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead t14 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t14.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t15 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t15.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t16 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t16.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include participants 18 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 18;

-- STEP 1.2: Include participants with diabetes (any of the three criteria)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes'
   OR f."take_diabetic_pills_to_lower_blood_sugar" = 'Yes'
   OR f."taking_insulin_now" = 'Yes';

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants with missing neutrophil counts
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."segmented_neutrophils_num_1000_cell_ul" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with missing lymphocyte counts
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."lymphocyte_number_1000_cells_ul" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with missing "have little interest in doing things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude participants with missing "feeling down, depressed, or hopeless"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude participants with missing "trouble sleeping or sleeping too much"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude participants with missing "feeling tired or having little energy"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude participants with missing "poor appetite or overeating"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude participants with missing "feeling bad about yourself"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.9: Exclude participants with missing "trouble concentrating on things"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step9 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.10: Exclude participants with missing "moving or speaking slowly or too fast"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step10 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step9 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.11: Exclude participants with missing "thought you would be better off dead"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step11 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step10 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step11;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Neutrophil-to-Lymphocyte Ratio (NLR)
-- Calculate NLR for cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_nlr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."segmented_neutrophils_num_1000_cell_ul" / f."lymphocyte_number_1000_cells_ul" AS "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for NLR within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_nlr_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio") AS q3
FROM temp_nlr;

-- Assign NLR quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_nlr_quartiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_nlr_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: ≤25%'
    WHEN "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_nlr_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: >25% and ≤50%'
    WHEN "neutrophil_lymphocyte_ratio" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_nlr_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: >50% and ≤75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: >75%'
  END AS "neutrophil_count_lymphocyte_count"
FROM temp_nlr;

-- 4.5 Depression Outcome
-- Map depression question responses to numeric scores
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_scores AS
SELECT 
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE f."have_little_interest_in_doing_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score1,
  CASE f."feeling_down_depressed_or_hopeless"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score2,
  CASE f."trouble_sleeping_or_sleeping_too_much"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score3,
  CASE f."feeling_tired_or_having_little_energy"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score4,
  CASE f."poor_appetite_or_overeating"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score5,
  CASE f."feeling_bad_about_yourself"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score6,
  CASE f."trouble_concentrating_on_things"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score7,
  CASE f."moving_or_speaking_slowly_or_too_fast"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score8,
  CASE f."thought_you_would_be_better_off_dead"
    WHEN 'Not at all' THEN 0
    WHEN 'Several days' THEN 1
    WHEN 'More than half the days' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Nearly every day' THEN 3
    ELSE 0
  END AS score9
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate total depression score and determine depression outcome
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_depression_outcome AS
SELECT 
  "respondent_sequence_number",
  (score1 + score2 + score3 + score4 + score5 + score6 + score7 + score8 + score9) AS total_score,
  CASE 
    WHEN (score1 + score2 + score3 + score4 + score5 + score6 + score7 + score8 + score9) >= 10 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "depression_outcome"
FROM temp_depression_scores;

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Output ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT 
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  a."age_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  n."neutrophil_count_lymphocyte_count",
  d."depression_outcome"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_nlr_quartiles n ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = n."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_depression_outcome d ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = d."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========

R:
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("depression_outcome")
    
    cohort <- df


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["depression_outcome"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(

    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")

    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")

    predictor_vars <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary", "numerical")]
    factor_candidates <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]

    # Convert binary/categorical variables to factor if not already
    for (var in factor_candidates) {
    if (!is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
    }

    # Initialize reference_levels table
    reference_levels <- tibble(variable = character(), reference_level = character())

    # Clean, trim, and set reference levels for all factor variables
    for (col in factor_candidates) {
    df[[col]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[col]])))
    levels_vec <- levels(df[[col]])

    # Try to find preferred reference
    preferred_order <- c("healthy", "none", "control", "reference", "ref", "normal","Q1", "low", "0", "1")
    matches <- tolower(levels_vec)  # lower case to standardize
    ref_level <- NA  # default

    for (keyword in preferred_order) {
    idx <- which(grepl(keyword, matches, ignore.case = TRUE))
    if (length(idx) > 0) {
        ref_level <- levels_vec[idx[1]]  # pick the first match for that keyword
        break  # stop at the highest-priority match
    }
    }


    # If none found, fall back to most common level
    if (is.na(ref_level)) {
        level_counts <- table(df[[col]])
        ref_level <- names(level_counts)[which.max(level_counts)]
    }

    # Set reference level
    df[[col]] <- relevel(df[[col]], ref = ref_level)

    # Record reference level
    reference_levels <- bind_rows(
        reference_levels,
        tibble(variable = col, reference_level = ref_level)
    )
    }
    
        
    
    x_raw <- df[[outcome_var]]

    y <- rep(NA_integer_, length(x_chr))

    y[x_chr %in% c("yes", "y", "true", "t")]  <- 1L
    y[x_chr %in% c("no",  "n", "false", "f")] <- 0L

    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_chr))
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 1] <- 1L
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 0] <- 0L

    idx <- is.na(y) & !is.na(x_chr)
    if (any(idx)) {
    vals <- sort(unique(x_chr[idx]))

    if (length(vals) != 2) {
        stop(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' must have exactly 2 non-missing classes after excluding none. Found: %s",
        outcome_var, paste(vals, collapse = ", ")
        ))
    }

    tab <- table(x_chr[idx])
    minority <- names(tab)[which.min(tab)]
    majority <- setdiff(vals, minority)

    cat(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' recoding: 1 = %s (minority)  0 = %s (majority)",
        outcome_var, minority, majority
    ))

    y[idx] <- as.integer(x_chr[idx] == minority)
    }

    df[[outcome_var]] <- y

    # Must contain both 0 and 1 somewhere (plus NA allowed)
    u <- sort(unique(df[[outcome_var]][!is.na(df[[outcome_var]])]))
    if (!(length(u) == 2 && all(u == c(0, 1)))) {
    stop(sprintf(
        "After recoding, '%s' must contain both 0 and 1 (plus NA for none). Found: %s",
        outcome_var,
        ifelse(length(u) == 0, "<no non-missing values>", paste(u, collapse = ", "))
    ))
    }

    formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + ")))
                
    
        
    
    model <- glm(formula,
                data = df,
                family =  binomial(link = "logit"))
            
    
    result_table <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
        select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)
